---
title: "Gutsy login screen resolution"
date: 2007-10-23
forum: General Help
---

### Post by amadeus266 on 2007-10-23
My Login screen is using the wrong resolution but once logged in it switches to the correct resolution (1152x860). Anyone know how to fix this?

Here is my xorg.conf
```
# xorg.conf (xorg X Window System server configuration file)
#
# This file was generated by dexconf, the Debian X Configuration tool, using
# values from the debconf database.
#
# Edit this file with caution, and see the xorg.conf manual page.
# (Type "man xorg.conf" at the shell prompt.)
#
# This file is automatically updated on xserver-xorg package upgrades *only*
# if it has not been modified since the last upgrade of the xserver-xorg
# package.
#
# If you have edited this file but would like it to be automatically updated
# again, run the following command:
#   sudo dpkg-reconfigure -phigh xserver-xorg

Section "Files"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Identifier	"Generic Keyboard"
	Driver		"kbd"
	Option		"CoreKeyboard"
	Option		"XkbRules"	"xorg"
	Option		"XkbModel"	"pc105"
	Option		"XkbLayout"	"us"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Identifier	"Configured Mouse"
	Driver		"mouse"
	Option		"CorePointer"
	Option		"Device"		"/dev/input/mice"
	Option		"Protocol"		"ImPS/2"
	Option		"ZAxisMapping"		"4 5"
	Option		"Emulate3Buttons"	"true"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Driver		"wacom"
	Identifier	"stylus"
	Option		"Device"	"/dev/input/wacom"
	Option		"Type"		"stylus"
	Option		"ForceDevice"	"ISDV4"		# Tablet PC ONLY
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Driver		"wacom"
	Identifier	"eraser"
	Option		"Device"	"/dev/input/wacom"
	Option		"Type"		"eraser"
	Option		"ForceDevice"	"ISDV4"		# Tablet PC ONLY
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Driver		"wacom"
	Identifier	"cursor"
	Option		"Device"	"/dev/input/wacom"
	Option		"Type"		"cursor"
	Option		"ForceDevice"	"ISDV4"		# Tablet PC ONLY
EndSection

Section "Device"
	Identifier	"ATI Technologies Inc RV280 [Radeon 9200]"
	Driver		"ati"
	BusID		"PCI:1:0:0"
EndSection

Section "Monitor"
	Identifier	"Compaq FS740"
	Option		"DPMS"
EndSection

Section "Screen"
	Identifier	"Default Screen"
	Device		"ATI Technologies Inc RV280 [Radeon 9200]"
	Monitor		"Compaq FS740"
	DefaultDepth	24
	SubSection "Display"
		Modes		"1280x1024" "1152x864" "1024x768" "800x600" "720x400" "640x480"
	EndSubSection
EndSection

Section "ServerLayout"
	Identifier	"Default Layout"
	Screen		"Default Screen"
	InputDevice	"Generic Keyboard"
	InputDevice	"Configured Mouse"

# Uncomment if you have a wacom tablet
#	InputDevice     "stylus"	"SendCoreEvents"
#	InputDevice     "cursor"	"SendCoreEvents"
#	InputDevice     "eraser"	"SendCoreEvents"
EndSection
```

Thanks in advance. Other than this and the fact that my tv tuner still doesn't work (probably never will due to age and the state of ATI's drivers for linux) I am loving this distro. Cheers to the developers.:guitar:

---

### Post by mannih2001 on 2007-10-24
Your preferred resolution is only the second in the list in xorg.conf. If you make it the first in the list, you might just get what you want.

---

### Post by Twiggy003 on 2007-11-11
My login screen has a similar problem, it goes to a weirdly high resolution that covers only half the screen, upon logging in it goes to the set 1280 x 1024, ive tried looking at the upslash conf, if that's relevant im not sure.

xorg bit:

Section "Screen"
	Identifier	"Default Screen"
	Device		"ATI Technologies Inc RV280 [Radeon 9200]"
	Monitor		"LCD-MONITOR"
	DefaultDepth	32
	SubSection "Display"
		Modes		"1280x1024" "1024x768" "832x624" "800x600" "720x400" "640x480"
	EndSubSection
EndSection

upslash:

# Usplash configuration file
xres=1280
yres=1024

any ideas? Thanks,

### Post by Damanther on 2007-11-13
If you change the order of the resolutions listed in your xorg.conf file so that the one you want for your login screen is the first on the list it should work.

---

### Post by tram on 2007-11-15
I managed to fix the splash screen issue...  My remaining problem is that the Login Window shows up in a very low resolution (800x600 I'm guessing).  

Once I login, the Desktop resolution is fine (1280x800).  I wouldn't care about this problem, but it also seems to be affecting the resolution of certain games (ie: supertux assaultcube etc...)  Every time I execute one of these games, they seem to revert back to the resolution in the login screen (800x600) which only covers part of the screen with the rest being black.

### Post by tram on 2007-11-25
I basically gave up (that's a new thing for me) because I'm just at a loss with this problem, and it's not crucial because the person using the computer probably won't be playing games at all.  The login screen resolution is an annoyance, but I just set it to auto login instead.  Not my preferred solution, but it's better than going back to Windows.

### Post by Strykaas on 2007-11-25
I seem to have fixed the login screen resolution by setting my preferred mode at the beginning of the modes list in xorg.conf file. I did not pay enough attention first, there are several modes lists, so consider each of them.

Strangely, the 1600x1024 bizarre mode that my login screen was using is not listed anywhere (especially in xorg.conf file). However, I have given a look at the xorg system logs in /var/... and I could see some kind of video card probing stuff where 1600x1024 mode was listed... 

To me this is clearly a regression compared to Ubuntu 7.04 as the login screen resolution was correctly managed from the start, and my hardware hasn't changed. It seems to me that this 7.10 version was not tested enough to get officially released, as these resolution troubles are nearly the most important things. Maybe I should contribute to beta tests and enter bugs ? :confused:

---

### Post by tram on 2007-11-26
If I recall corectly, I tried putting my prefered mode at the top in xorg.conf like you suggested, but the changes don't stick.  (yes, I was root) It's like it gets re-written back to what it was when it starts up..  I'll try again when I get home though, just to make sure.

I agree though.  As much as I love Ubuntu, this is an issue that really shouldn't exist, and it seems to be a widespread issue with a lot of users being affected (from what I'm reading on the forums).  I had no problems with previous versions of Ubuntu..

I only wish I had the time/skills to contribute to bug fixes.  I try to post my findings to help others whenever I can.

### Post by Strykaas on 2007-11-26
I still haven't figured it out correctly but I can confirm that strange things do occur with xorg.conf.
What I have seen so far is that there are two places in the main desktop menu (administration and preferences) where you can set the resolution. :confused:
And when you set the 'preferences' one, then  xorg.conf is rewritten completely, with correct monitor detection. Till then, my monitor was not correctly detected and mentioned in xorg.conf. How strange is that ?

I have to reinstall from scratch to *confirm* this, but there is obviousy an issue there.

I am glad not to have toasted my 650 eur monitor though. 1600x1024 for a login screen...

I have no idea why they work on compiz and other 'secondary' stuff while these issues remain. They try to attract new users with sexy compiz vids but when these come and try, they're really frustrated with these resolution pbs. Mark ? Your strategy lol ?

Second, given all the bad feedback posted through forums, why can't I just have an 'auto update' which addresses the issue. Why no SP ? Is this issue that complicated ?

Later alligator.

---

### Post by tram on 2007-11-27
I appreciate what they tried to do with the GUI mode when xorg.conf breaks (because a lot of users don't know how to edit manually), but it's not quite detecting hardware properly and fails to make the appropriate changes in xorg.

With my experiences so far, it is terribly poor at detecting old CRT monitors properly.  I wonder if they keep updating their ISO files with fixes, or do they simply propagate them via system updates. ### Post by amadeus266 on 2007-12-11
O.K. I have tried all the suggestions given so far but to no avail. Everything is working except the odd resolution problem that only exists with the login screen. It is just an annoyance so I will learn to live with it until I find a way to fix it. I will continue to watch this thread and hope for a fix though. A piece of advise to share though... Don't install Ubuntu while the system is connected to your monitor through a KVM switch. Your monitor will not be detected and you will more than likely end up with bigger problems than an incorrect resolution.

Meanwhile, I have relocated my computer and when I reconnected my KVM switch, My xorg.conf file was changed. Maybe someone can look at this version and come up with an idea to fix the login screen...```
Section "Files"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Identifier	"Generic Keyboard"
	Driver		"kbd"
	Option		"CoreKeyboard"
	Option		"XkbRules"	"xorg"
	Option		"XkbModel"	"pc105"
	Option		"XkbLayout"	"us"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Identifier	"Configured Mouse"
	Driver		"mouse"
	Option		"CorePointer"
	Option		"Device"	"/dev/input/mice"
	Option		"Protocol"	"ImPS/2"
	Option		"ZAxisMapping"	"4 5"
	Option		"Emulate3Buttons"	"true"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Driver		"wacom"
	Identifier	"stylus"
	Option		"Device"	"/dev/input/wacom"
	Option		"Type"	"stylus"
	Option		"ForceDevice"	"ISDV4"# Tablet PC ONLY
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Driver		"wacom"
	Identifier	"eraser"
	Option		"Device"	"/dev/input/wacom"
	Option		"Type"	"eraser"
	Option		"ForceDevice"	"ISDV4"# Tablet PC ONLY
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Driver		"wacom"
	Identifier	"cursor"
	Option		"Device"	"/dev/input/wacom"
	Option		"Type"	"cursor"
	Option		"ForceDevice"	"ISDV4"# Tablet PC ONLY
EndSection

Section "Device"
	Identifier	"ATI Technologies Inc RV280 [Radeon 9200]"
	Boardname	"ati"
	Busid		"PCI:1:0:0"
	Driver		"ati"
	Screen	0
	Option		"MergedFB"	"off"
EndSection

Section "Monitor"
	Identifier	"Compaq FS740"
	Vendorname	"Plug 'n' Play"
	Modelname	"Plug 'n' Play"
  modeline  "640x480@60" 25.2 640 656 752 800 480 490 492 525 -vsync -hsync
  modeline  "640x480@72" 31.5 640 664 704 832 480 489 491 520 -vsync -hsync
  modeline  "640x480@75" 31.5 640 656 720 840 480 481 484 500 -vsync -hsync
  modeline  "640x480@85" 36.0 640 696 752 832 480 481 484 509 -vsync -hsync
  modeline  "800x600@56" 36.0 800 824 896 1024 600 601 603 625 +hsync +vsync
  modeline  "800x600@72" 50.0 800 856 976 1040 600 637 643 666 +hsync +vsync
  modeline  "800x600@75" 49.5 800 816 896 1056 600 601 604 625 +hsync +vsync
  modeline  "800x600@85" 56.3 800 832 896 1048 600 601 604 631 +hsync +vsync
  modeline  "800x600@60" 40.0 800 840 968 1056 600 601 605 628 +hsync +vsync
  modeline  "832x624@75" 57.284 832 864 928 1152 624 625 628 667 -vsync -hsync
  modeline  "1024x768@85" 94.5 1024 1072 1168 1376 768 769 772 808 +hsync +vsync
  modeline  "1024x768@75" 78.8 1024 1040 1136 1312 768 769 772 800 +hsync +vsync
  modeline  "1024x768@70" 75.0 1024 1048 1184 1328 768 771 777 806 -vsync -hsync
  modeline  "1024x768@60" 65.0 1024 1048 1184 1344 768 771 777 806 -vsync -hsync
  modeline  "1024x768@43" 44.9 1024 1032 1208 1264 768 768 776 817 +hsync interlace +vsync
  modeline  "1152x864@75" 108.0 1152 1216 1344 1600 864 865 868 900 +hsync +vsync
  modeline  "1280x960@60" 102.1 1280 1360 1496 1712 960 961 964 994 -hsync +vsync
  modeline  "1280x1024@60" 108.0 1280 1328 1440 1688 1024 1025 1028 1066 +hsync +vsync
  modeline  "1400x1050@60" 122.61 1400 1488 1640 1880 1050 1051 1054 1087 -hsync +vsync
	Gamma	1.0
EndSection

Section "Screen"
	Identifier	"Default Screen"
	Device		"ATI Technologies Inc RV280 [Radeon 9200]"
	Monitor		"Compaq FS740"
	Defaultdepth	24
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth	24
		Virtual	1400	1050
		Modes		"1024x768@85"	"1024x768@75"	"832x624@75"	"1024x768@70"	"800x600@60"	"1024x768@60"	"800x600@85"	"1024x768@43"	"800x600@75"	"1152x864@75"	"800x600@72"	"1280x960@60"	"800x600@56"	"1280x1024@60"	"640x480@85"	"1400x1050@60"	"640x480@75"	"640x480@72"	"640x480@60"
	EndSubSection
EndSection

Section "ServerLayout"
	Identifier	"Default Layout"
  screen 0 "Default Screen" 0 0
	Inputdevice	"Generic Keyboard"
	Inputdevice	"Configured Mouse"
	
	# Uncomment if you have a wacom tablet
	#	InputDevice     "stylus"	"SendCoreEvents"
	#	InputDevice     "cursor"	"SendCoreEvents"
	#	InputDevice     "eraser"	"SendCoreEvents"
EndSection
Section "Module"
	Load		"glx"
	Load		"GLcore"
	Load		"v4l"
EndSection
Section "device" #  
	Identifier	"device1"
	Boardname	"ati"
	Busid		"PCI:1:0:0"
	Driver		"ati"
	Screen	1
	Option		"MergedFB"	"off"
EndSection
Section "screen" #  
	Identifier	"screen1"
	Device		"device1"
	Defaultdepth	24
	Monitor		"monitor1"
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth	24
		Modes		"640x480@60"	"640x480@72"	"640x480@75"	"640x480@85"	"800x600@56"	"800x600@72"	"800x600@75"	"800x600@85"	"800x600@60"	"832x624@75"	"1024x768@85"	"1024x768@75"	"1024x768@70"	"1024x768@60"	"1024x768@43"	"1152x864@75"	"1280x960@60"	"1280x1024@60"	"1400x1050@60"
	EndSubSection
EndSection
Section "monitor" #  
	Identifier	"monitor1"
	Vendorname	"Plug 'n' Play"
	Modelname	"Plug 'n' Play"
  modeline  "640x480@60" 25.2 640 656 752 800 480 490 492 525 -vsync -hsync
  modeline  "640x480@72" 31.5 640 664 704 832 480 489 491 520 -vsync -hsync
  modeline  "640x480@75" 31.5 640 656 720 840 480 481 484 500 -vsync -hsync
  modeline  "640x480@85" 36.0 640 696 752 832 480 481 484 509 -vsync -hsync
  modeline  "800x600@56" 36.0 800 824 896 1024 600 601 603 625 +hsync +vsync
  modeline  "800x600@72" 50.0 800 856 976 1040 600 637 643 666 +hsync +vsync
  modeline  "800x600@75" 49.5 800 816 896 1056 600 601 604 625 +hsync +vsync
  modeline  "800x600@85" 56.3 800 832 896 1048 600 601 604 631 +hsync +vsync
  modeline  "800x600@60" 40.0 800 840 968 1056 600 601 605 628 +hsync +vsync
  modeline  "832x624@75" 57.284 832 864 928 1152 624 625 628 667 -vsync -hsync
  modeline  "1024x768@85" 94.5 1024 1072 1168 1376 768 769 772 808 +hsync +vsync
  modeline  "1024x768@75" 78.8 1024 1040 1136 1312 768 769 772 800 +hsync +vsync
  modeline  "1024x768@70" 75.0 1024 1048 1184 1328 768 771 777 806 -vsync -hsync
  modeline  "1024x768@60" 65.0 1024 1048 1184 1344 768 771 777 806 -vsync -hsync
  modeline  "1024x768@43" 44.9 1024 1032 1208 1264 768 768 776 817 +hsync interlace +vsync
  modeline  "1152x864@75" 108.0 1152 1216 1344 1600 864 865 868 900 +hsync +vsync
  modeline  "1280x960@60" 102.1 1280 1360 1496 1712 960 961 964 994 -hsync +vsync

  modeline  "1400x1050@60" 122.61 1400 1488 1640 1880 1050 1051 1054 1087 -hsync +vsync
	Gamma	1.0
EndSection
Section "ServerFlags"
EndSection
```

I wonder if it has something to do with the vsync and hsync values being incorrect. Any ideas?

### Post by lil_B on 2007-12-11
Just for the sake of experimentation...

Comment out ( # ) all references to the offending resolutions
Try commenting out "Virtual	1400	1050"

For some reason the login screen always seems to pick the highest resolution it can... I have had this problem so many times... 
This is how I fix it. I hope it works for you.

---

### Post by Strykaas on 2007-12-11
> **lil_B said:**
> 
For some reason the login screen always seems to pick the highest resolution it can... I have had this problem so many times... 


It occured to me only on GG, not FF. I would comment the unwanted resolutions as well.

PLUS, I would alter the TWO lines begining with "Modes" (within SubSection Display) by placing the first wanted resolution at first place in the list. That should make it hopefully.

---

### Post by Strykaas on 2008-05-07
8.04 sorted out all my problems. Thank you xorg.
